ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Vanilla Cream Frappuccino; closest parent 'blended coffee beverage' (FOODON_03301710) used as the nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Coffee-based beverages) is the closest parent category; no specific Frappuccino code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 'Coffee drink' is the closest available parent; no specific blended vanilla cream coffee beverage code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard tall (12 fl oz / 355 ml) serving of Vanilla Cream Frappuccino as served at Starbucks Singapore without whipped cream.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Vanilla Frappuccino Chilled Coffee Drink — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Starbucks Vanilla Frappuccino chilled coffee drink (FDC ID 2103695), which is a vanilla-flavoured blended milk-and-coffee beverage comparable in composition to the Vanilla Cream Frappuccino served without whipped cream. Values are expressed per 100 g. The Singapore Starbucks version is served without whipped cream, consistent with the USDA reference product formulation. Minor differences in exact sugar or fat content may exist between markets; values are treated as best available estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Starbucks Vanilla Cream Frappuccino. GI of 55 is estimated by analogy to sweetened low-fat flavoured milk beverages and vanilla-flavoured milk drinks, which are documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) with GI values in the range 40–60. A midpoint estimate of 55 (low GI) is applied. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 51.5) / 100 ≈ 28.3, rounded to 28.2. Serving carbohydrate estimated as 14.5 g per 100 g × 355 g serving = 51.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~55 mg per 100 g; ~195 mg per tall serving)
hypertension.guidance: The sodium content of a single tall serving (~195 mg) is modest and unlikely to significantly impact blood pressure in most hypertensive individuals, but regular daily consumption of multiple servings should be monitored within overall dietary sodium targets of less than 2000 mg per day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content (~13.5 g per 100 g; ~48 g per tall serving) and low glycemic index (~55)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this beverage due to its high free sugar load (~48 g per tall serving) and low GI of approximately 55, which can still contribute to a postprandial blood glucose rise; consumption should be limited or replaced with unsweetened alternatives.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~1.0 g per 100 g) and high added sugar content (~48 g per tall serving)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While the saturated fat content per serving is relatively low (~3.6 g per tall serving), the high added sugar content contributes to excess caloric intake and may adversely affect triglyceride levels and cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake to occasional consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Vanilla Cream Frappuccino is a chilled, blended coffee-shop beverage sold by Starbucks, including at Starbucks outlets in Singapore such as Starbucks One Fullerton. It is made from a blend of vanilla syrup, low-fat milk, and ice, producing a smooth, creamy, and sweet frozen drink with a pronounced vanilla flavour and a milkshake-like texture. The beverage is served without whipped cream in the Singapore market. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 79 kcal, 2.5 g protein, 1.5 g fat (of which 1.0 g is saturated), and 14.5 g carbohydrate, of which approximately 13.5 g is sugar. A standard tall serving (355 ml / approximately 355 g) delivers roughly 280 kcal and 48 g of free sugars, making it a high-sugar beverage. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low GI) by analogy to sweetened flavoured low-fat milk beverages. Clinically, this drink is of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its high free sugar load and low GI, and for those with cardiovascular disease due to its contribution to excess caloric and sugar intake. It is vegetarian and gluten-free but contains dairy (milk allergen). It is classified as an ultra-processed beverage. In Singapore, it is available via field survey from Starbucks outlets and is priced at approximately SGD 8.50 per serving.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Starbucks Vanilla Cream Frappuccino?
Is Vanilla Cream Frappuccino suitable for diabetics?
How much sugar is in a Vanilla Frappuccino?
Is Starbucks Vanilla Frappuccino gluten free?
Vanilla Cream Frappuccino nutrition facts Singapore
What is the GI of a Vanilla Frappuccino?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from the closest available USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for a Starbucks vanilla-flavoured blended milk beverage. The Singapore Starbucks Vanilla Cream Frappuccino is not directly listed in any approved primary nutritional database (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Foundation Foods, or ICMR-NIN). The USDA Branded Foods reference is the highest-quality approved source available for this commercial product. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database category for sweetened flavoured milk beverages. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods (FDC ID 2103695, Starbucks Vanilla Frappuccino chilled coffee drink) as the closest approved source. The Singapore market version is served without whipped cream, consistent with the reference product. Minor formulation differences between markets may exist. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Foods source entry for this product. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 3. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g per 100 g; this is confirmed zero per the USDA source for this product category. Dietary fibre is 0.0 g per 100 g; confirmed zero per the USDA source as this is a liquid blended beverage with no fibre-contributing solid ingredients. Vitamin C is 0.0 mg per 100 g; confirmed zero per the USDA source. 4. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on sweetened flavoured low-fat milk beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021); no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. 5. The food_group has been corrected from the input value 'Mixed' to 'Beverages' as this is a blended liquid beverage. 6. Multilingual local names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are not applicable for this food as it is a Western commercial coffee-shop beverage of US origin sold globally under an English brand name; no established local-language names exist in the Singaporean context beyond the English brand name; G6 multilingual compliance is not triggered as this is not a Singaporean dish — it is a US-origin commercial branded beverage served in Singapore. 7. The registered trademark symbol (®) has been removed from the canonical food_name per Veritas naming conventions; it is retained in aliases.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected food_group from 'Mixed' (input) to 'Beverages' (correct classification for a blended liquid beverage).
Removed registered trademark symbol from canonical food_name; retained in aliases.
Added full nutrient profile adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods.
Added GI estimate (55, low) from analogous sweetened flavoured milk beverage category per University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. (2021).
Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for glycemic_index 55, and updated associated notes and health context guidance.
Added glycemic load per serving calculation (28.2) based on 355 g serving.
Added ontology anchors for FoodOn, FoodEx2, and SNOMED CT using closest_parent match type with gap notes.
Added health context for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease with specific clinical guidance.
Added LLM-ready natural language description exceeding 200 characters.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) in notes.
Documented all 0.0 values (trans_fat_g, dietary_fibre_g, vitamin_c_mg) with confirmed zero justification in notes.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent.

Confirmed G6 multilingual compliance: this is a US-origin commercial branded beverage, not a Singaporean/Japanese/Korean/Indonesian/Thai/Vietnamese dish, so mandatory local-language names do not apply.
